sources.writer_profile: Pepe Monforte is the founding director of CosasDeCome Sevilla, a local food and restaurant guide that has covered Seville's gastronomic scene for over two decades. He is one of the most respected food voices in the city, organising the annual Solera awards with a panel of 70 industry experts and writing detailed neighbourhood dining guides that are widely used by locals. His January 2026 guide to Triana and Los Remedios was one of the most recent comprehensive reviews of that area's restaurant scene.

Pepe Monforte is the founding director of CosasDeCome Sevilla, a local food and restaurant guide that has covered Seville's gastronomic scene for over two decades. His April 2024 guide to the 30 best tapas bars in Triana and Los Remedios draws on reader nominations and editorial expertise to identify the most loved neighbourhood bars.

Pepe Monforte is the founding director of CosasDeCome Sevilla. His February 2026 guide to dining in Nervión covers 50 restaurants across one of Seville's most commercially active and densely populated districts, ranked by gastronomic interest.

Pepe Monforte is the founding director of CosasDeCome Sevilla. His July 2025 guide to Pino Montano and San Jerónimo covers two adjacent northern neighbourhoods that are well-populated with a rich and varied restaurant offering, often overlooked by visitors.

Ariana Buenafuente is a staff writer (redactora) at Sevilla Secreta (sevillasecreta.co), a local city guide written for Seville residents rather than tourists. The publication covers everything from neighbourhood secrets to celebrity dining spots, and Buenafuente's November 2023 article on Seville's restaurant scene focused on establishments frequented by international celebrities visiting the city.

Ariana Buenafuente is a staff writer at Sevilla Secreta. Her August 2024 article identified seven restaurants that remained open during August, when many Seville restaurants close for summer holidays.

Brenda Alonso is a food and lifestyle writer for The Objective, an independent Spanish digital media outlet covering culture, society, and gastronomy. Her July 2023 guide to where to eat in Seville covered 16 restaurants across multiple cuisines and neighbourhoods, with a particular emphasis on international dining options alongside traditional Andalusian fare.

Mari Carmen Duarte is a journalist specialising in travel and gastronomy for Viajes National Geographic España. Her February 2025 article on eight restaurants and gastronomic corners in central Seville focused on innovative and under-the-radar dining experiences that go beyond the city's most famous tapas bars, including Europe's first omakase pizza bar and a Michelin-recognised omakase Japanese counter.

Shawn Hennessey is a Canadian-born food writer who has lived in Seville since 1993. He runs Bitesize Sevilla, a Substack newsletter and website (azahar-sevilla.com) that he describes as 'probably the most complete guide in English for tapas bars and restaurants in Sevilla,' with approximately 300 places reviewed. His reviews are editorially independent with no advertising and no free meals accepted. His December 2025 piece on the year's top new restaurant openings is one of the most current independent assessments of Seville's dining scene.

Lauren Aloise is a trained chef and food writer based in Seville, and the creator of Spanish Sabores, a food blog covering Spanish cuisine and local dining. She is also co-founder of Devour Tours, a food tour company operating in Seville, which represents a commercial conflict of interest. Her April 2024 guide to eating in Seville draws on years of personal experience living in the city.
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour company operating in Seville

restaurants.name: Castizo
restaurants.signature_dish: Tosta of Almadraba tuna with mustard mayo and truffle; chickpeas with cabbage; cazón in adobo; brioche with béchamel
restaurants.quote: Aquí hay una barra de las de antes, con tapas de siempre y guisos de toda la vida — a classic bar with traditional tapas and age-old stews; also noted for brioche with Iberian pork jowl and green asparagus Cádiz-style carbonara.

restaurants.name: De la O
restaurants.quote: Located next to the Puente de la Expiración with views of the Guadalquivir; created in 2018 as an ode to local seasonal product and to Triana's pottery roots.

restaurants.name: Eterno
restaurants.quote: Top creative cuisine skilfully executed, beautifully presented and professionally served; opened August 2025 at Plaza Venerables in Barrio Santa Cruz.

restaurants.name: El Eslava
restaurants.signature_dish: Croquetas de choco y alioli negro; carrillón de cerdo ibérico; coquinas; fried ortiguillas
restaurants.quote: Uno de los restaurantes más populares de Sevilla — known to many as the best tapas bar in Seville, this tiny classic bar has kept its prices very low and the quality high; signature coquetas de choco and carrillón de cerdo ibérico.

restaurants.name: Las Golondrinas
restaurants.signature_dish: Marinated vegetables, punta de solomillo, chipirones a la plancha, mushrooms with alioli
restaurants.quote: Hidden away in Triana's ceramics district, this classic spot is known for its delicious and simple tapas; seven reader recommendations — highest rated bar in CosasDeCome's Triana tapas guide.

restaurants.name: Casa Morales
restaurants.signature_dish: Montaditos de pringá, salt cod with salmorejo, tortilla de patatas, lomo en manteca
restaurants.quote: Historic bar on Seville's seafaring García de Vinuesa street in El Arenal; one of Seville's most beloved old-fashioned bars — entering it feels like traveling back in time.

restaurants.name: Bodeguita Romero
restaurants.signature_dish: Pringá sandwiches, spinach and chickpeas, secreto and presa pork cuts
restaurants.quote: One of Seville's best classic tapas bars; signature pringá sandwiches, spinach and chickpeas, and secreto and presa pork cuts on Calle Harinas; described as opción económica pero deliciosa.

restaurants.name: Sr. Cangrejo
restaurants.signature_dish: Barrita Brava, corn aguachile with white shrimp and avocado, crab chawanmushi
restaurants.quote: Una alta cocina que se apoya tanto en los detalles de pinza como en el cuchareo de fondos — haute cuisine balancing precision with depth; one Michelin star; signature corn aguachile with white shrimp and avocado and crab chawanmushi.

restaurants.name: Kinu
restaurants.signature_dish: Nigiri with wild sea bream, turbot, aged tuna; fresh wasabi; dashi soup with Andalusian influences
restaurants.quote: Behind a noren curtain and descending stairs, Kinu offers a 15-course omakase for six diners maximum; nigiri made with wild sea bream, turbot, and aged tuna; Seville's first omakase bar.

restaurants.name: ENEKO Basque Sevilla
restaurants.signature_dish: Hake tempura with green sauce, foie cuajada with citronella, goxua with caramel ice cream, farm egg yolk with wheat stew
restaurants.quote: Sus raíces culinarias pero con el producto al que está acostumbrada la ciudad — Basque culinary roots using the local Andalusian products; Michelin-listed; located at Plaza de la Magdalena.

restaurants.name: Ricca
restaurants.signature_dish: Six-course omakase pizza; antipasti; dessert
restaurants.quote: Considered Europe's first omakase pizza bar; eight diners at a time experience antipasti, six pizza portions, and dessert cooked in Forni Valoriani ovens from Florence.

restaurants.name: Casa Orzáez
restaurants.signature_dish: Artisanal family-made cheese, garbanzos stew, beef round with wheat and vegetables, fermented products
restaurants.quote: Una oda a la gastronomía — an ode to gastronomy; four siblings run this slow-food restaurant with family-made artisanal cheeses from their Sierra Norte workshop, fermented products, and garbanzos stew on Calle Betis.

restaurants.name: Casa Regina
restaurants.quote: Great traditional food and service, as always; opened April 2025 next to the Encarnación Market.

restaurants.name: Don Jorge
restaurants.quote: A combination of traditional dishes with a few fusiony twists while staying connected to market fresh products; opened May 2025 near Triana market.

restaurants.name: Salpicón
restaurants.quote: A great independent tapas bar has opened in Barrio Santa Cruz with seasonal local products; opened August 2025.

restaurants.name: Las Golondrinas Centro
restaurants.quote: One of the best-loved tapas bars in town opens its city-centre location; the Arcas family, Triana legends, set up shop in the heart of the city just off Plaza Nueva in December 2025.

restaurants.name: María Trifulca
restaurants.quote: Refined views to the Guadalquivir and Seville's monumental riverside make it an excellent draw; Triana riverfront restaurant with an emphasis on mariscos and fish.

restaurants.name: Bodeguita Antonio Romero
restaurants.signature_dish: Piripis (house signature), croquetas, montaditos
restaurants.quote: Specialty croquetas, house-signature piripis, and montaditos; a family tapas bar in El Arenal noted by Sevilla Secreta for its authenticity and celebrity clientele during the Latin Grammy Awards.

restaurants.name: La Casa del Tigre
restaurants.quote: One of the most interesting restaurants in Seville for enjoying haute cuisine without pretension or constraints; at kilometre zero of the city.

restaurants.name: Marabunda
restaurants.quote: Listed by Sevilla Secreta as one of seven Seville restaurants open in August; located at c/ Jesús del Gran Poder, 31 in La Macarena.

restaurants.name: La Vicenta
restaurants.quote: Listed by Sevilla Secreta as one of seven Seville restaurants open in August; located at c/ Santa Vicenta María, 7 in the historic centre.

restaurants.name: Tradevo
restaurants.signature_dish: Tataki de presa ibérica con mojo picón, alcachofas rellenas de rabo de toro
restaurants.quote: Tradition meets evolution; signature tataki de presa ibérica con mojo picón and alcachofas rellenas de rabo de toro; founded 2010, now with two locations in Nervión and Centro.

restaurants.name: La Bernarda
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo con helado de aceite de oliva
restaurants.quote: Traditional Sevillian cuisine with a creative touch; signature salmorejo con helado de aceite de oliva; a gastrobar with contemporary setting and market-fresh cuisine that blends tradition with haute cuisine.
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Calle La Casa de Bernarda Alba

restaurants.name: Zelai
restaurants.signature_dish: Bacalao a la vizcaína, txuletón de buey
restaurants.quote: Basque restaurant on Calle Albareda in the Arenal; signature bacalao a la vizcaína and txuletón de buey.

restaurants.name: La Gorda de las Delicias
restaurants.signature_dish: Gambas al ajillo, secreto ibérico a la parrilla con chimichurri
restaurants.quote: Acogedor restaurante que rinde homenaje a la rica tradición culinaria de Andalucía — an inviting restaurant honouring Andalusian culinary tradition; signature gambas al ajillo and secreto ibérico with chimichurri on Paseo de las Delicias.

restaurants.name: Depikofino
restaurants.signature_dish: Tartar de atún con aguacate y soja
restaurants.quote: Fusion restaurant on Avenida Eduardo Dato in Nervión; signature tartar de atún con aguacate y soja.

restaurants.name: Sibuya
restaurants.signature_dish: Sushi variado, fideos udon
restaurants.quote: Asian restaurant on Calle Albareda in the Arenal area; signature sushi variado and fideos udon.

restaurants.name: Torres y García
restaurants.signature_dish: Carrillera ibérica con parmentier trufado, tartar de langostinos
restaurants.quote: La tradición se mezcla con la modernidad en cada plato — tradition mixed with modernity in every dish; signature carrillera ibérica con parmentier trufado and tartar de langostinos on Calle Harinas.

restaurants.name: La Taberna del Arenal
restaurants.signature_dish: Rabo de toro, carrillada al Pedro Ximénez
restaurants.quote: Un lugar con encanto e historia near Plaza de Toros — a place of charm and history near the bullring; signature rabo de toro and carrillada al Pedro Ximénez.

restaurants.name: Casa Rafel
restaurants.quote: Listed among the recommended restaurants of Triana and Los Remedios in Pepe Monforte's January 2026 CosasDeCome guide; price range €30–40.

restaurants.name: Victoria 8
restaurants.signature_dish: Rabo de toro
restaurants.quote: Signature rabo de toro; listed among the top restaurants of Triana and Los Remedios in Pepe Monforte's January 2026 CosasDeCome guide; price range €30–40.

restaurants.name: Arrocería Criaito
restaurants.signature_dish: Arroz con gambas
restaurants.quote: Lunch-only rice specialist; signature arroz con gambas; listed by Pepe Monforte among the best restaurants of Triana and Los Remedios in January 2026.

restaurants.name: Bar Amarra
restaurants.signature_dish: Cazón en tomate
restaurants.quote: Signature cazón en tomate; three reader recommendations in CosasDeCome's Triana tapas guide; featured in both the neighbourhood dining guide and tapas bar guide by Pepe Monforte.

restaurants.name: La Montanera
restaurants.quote: Two reader recommendations in CosasDeCome's tapas guide; featured in both Pepe Monforte's Triana dining guide and tapas bar guide; open Wednesday to Sunday.

restaurants.name: Restaurante Jaylu
restaurants.quote: Price range €50+; five reader recommendations; recognised with 1 Sol Guía Repsol; listed among the finest restaurants of Triana and Los Remedios by Pepe Monforte.

restaurants.name: El Mercader de Triana
restaurants.quote: A specialist wine bar in Triana with original specialities and a tasting room called La Tintorería; listed among the top bars of Triana by Pepe Monforte and voted among Sevilla's best wine bars.

restaurants.name: La Reserva de Joaquín Márquez
restaurants.quote: Listed in Pepe Monforte's January 2026 CosasDeCome guide to the best restaurants in Triana and Los Remedios; price range €30–40.

restaurants.name: Alfarería 21
restaurants.quote: Six reader recommendations — among the most-endorsed bars in CosasDeCome's guide to the best tapas bars in Triana and Los Remedios; open daily for lunch and dinner.

restaurants.name: Bar Casa Casimiro
restaurants.quote: Three reader recommendations; listed in CosasDeCome's guide to the best tapas bars in Triana and Los Remedios by Pepe Monforte.

restaurants.name: Bar Juan Carlos
restaurants.quote: A cheese paradise serving over 100 varieties; listed in CosasDeCome's 30-bar tapas guide to Triana and Los Remedios.

restaurants.name: Besana Tapas Sevilla
restaurants.quote: Two reader recommendations; listed in CosasDeCome's guide to the best tapas bars in Triana and Los Remedios by Pepe Monforte; open for lunch and dinner Tuesday through Sunday.

restaurants.name: D Culto
restaurants.quote: Price range €40–50; listed among the top restaurants of Nervión by Pepe Monforte in his February 2026 CosasDeCome guide to the area's 50 best restaurants.

restaurants.name: Iki
restaurants.quote: Price range €50+; Japanese restaurant in Nervión near the Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán stadium; listed in Pepe Monforte's February 2026 guide to the best restaurants in Nervión.

restaurants.name: Keycha Placer Ibérico
restaurants.quote: Price range €40–50; Iberian pork specialist in Nervión; listed among the top restaurants in the area in Pepe Monforte's February 2026 CosasDeCome guide.

restaurants.name: Puerta Caleta
restaurants.quote: Price range €20–30; daily service except Sunday evenings and Mondays; listed in Pepe Monforte's February 2026 guide to quality dining in Nervión.

restaurants.name: Restaurante Mara
restaurants.quote: Price range €30–40; daily service; listed in Pepe Monforte's February 2026 CosasDeCome guide to the best restaurants in Nervión.

restaurants.name: Tarantelo Tapas
restaurants.signature_dish: Almadraba red tuna preparations
restaurants.quote: A project of siblings Mercedes and Enrique Rico with a defined identity — devotion to red almadraba tuna; serves Tuesday to Sunday in continuous lunch-to-dinner service in Pino Montano.

restaurants.name: Athenea Bar
restaurants.signature_dish: Tostada pringa con queso
restaurants.quote: Signature tostada pringa con queso; listed in Pepe Monforte's July 2025 guide to dining in Pino Montano and San Jerónimo.

restaurants.name: Mesón Las Torres
restaurants.signature_dish: Ensaladilla de pulpo
restaurants.quote: Signature ensaladilla de pulpo (octopus salad); listed in CosasDeCome's July 2025 guide to the best places to eat in Pino Montano and San Jerónimo.

restaurants.name: Bar La Paradita
restaurants.signature_dish: Menudo
restaurants.quote: Signature menudo (tripe stew); listed in CosasDeCome's July 2025 guide to dining in Pino Montano and San Jerónimo by Pepe Monforte.

restaurants.name: Brasería La Tremenda
restaurants.quote: Grilled meats specialist in Pino Montano; listed in Pepe Monforte's July 2025 CosasDeCome guide; open for lunch and dinner daily except Wednesdays.

restaurants.name: Bodega Dos de Mayo
restaurants.quote: The quintessential Seville tapas bar loved by locals and tourists alike; located on Plaza de la Gavidia in the historic centre.

restaurants.name: La Azotea
restaurants.signature_dish: Seasonal changing menu
restaurants.quote: You can instantly tell there is true passion in the kitchen; modern tapas bar on Calle Jesús del Gran Poder with a seasonal changing menu.

restaurants.name: La Brunilda
restaurants.quote: A delicious husband-and-wife-owned and operated tapas bar on Calle Galera in El Arenal with a respectable wine list.

restaurants.name: Casa Moreno
restaurants.signature_dish: Montaditos
restaurants.quote: One of Seville's many abacerías — part grocery store, part bar — easy to brush off as a tiny store from the outside but a Seville institution on Calle Gamazo.

restaurants.name: El Rinconcillo
restaurants.signature_dish: Espinacas con garbanzos
restaurants.quote: The oldest bar in Seville, founded in 1670 — El Rinconcillo is a can't-miss; signature espinacas con garbanzos.

restaurants.name: Freiduría La Isla
restaurants.signature_dish: Fried fish, calamares del campo, cazón
restaurants.quote: Seville is famous for fried fish, and my favorite comes from La Isla; signature calamares del campo and cazón on Calle García de Vinuesa.

restaurants.name: Blanco Cerrillo
restaurants.signature_dish: Boquerones fritos
restaurants.quote: No visit to Seville is complete without popping into Blanco Cerrillo; signature boquerones fritos (fried anchovies) paired with manzanilla.

restaurants.name: La Taberna
restaurants.signature_dish: Rice of the day, grilled grouper and vegetable skewer
restaurants.quote: Another of Seville's best traditional tapas bars, La Taberna is run by Maria and Miguel; signature rice of the day and grilled grouper and vegetables skewer on Calle Gamazo.

restaurants.name: Bodega Santa Cruz (Las Columnas)
restaurants.signature_dish: Montadito de pringá
restaurants.quote: One of the most classic bars on the border of the Santa Cruz neighborhood; signature montadito de pringá; they still take note of your bill in chalk on the bar.

restaurants.name: Taberna Águilas
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo, montaditos
restaurants.quote: One of Seville's most traditional small tapas bars; signature salmorejo and montaditos on Calle Águilas.
